pages.rationale: This page is the cover of the 'Economy PT 365' document, providing the title and publication period. Cover pages are not suitable for question generation.
This page presents the initial sections of the Table of Contents for the document. Table of Contents pages are not suitable for question generation.
This page continues the Table of Contents from the previous page, listing further topics and subtopics. Table of Contents pages are not suitable for question generation.
This page contains an introductory 'Note to Students' describing the document's features and a full copyright notice. It is not core content for question generation.

pages.subtopic: RBI Monetary Policy and Economic Capital Framework
pages.rationale: This page provides detailed explanations of RBI's monetary policy instruments (quantitative and qualitative tools), the recent surplus transfer to the government, and the Economic Capital Framework (ECF), including its genesis, concept, and components. This is highly relevant and dense content for UPSC.

pages.subtopic: Forex Reserves and RBI Ombudsman Scheme
pages.rationale: This page continues the discussion on RBI's Economic Capital Framework, then delves into India's Forex reserves, explaining the components, reasons for increasing gold reserves, and associated risks. It also introduces the Reserve Bank - Integrated Ombudsman Scheme, 2021, covering its objective and expanded coverage. This is highly dense and relevant content.

pages.subtopic: RBI Ombudsman Scheme and Key Initiatives
pages.rationale: This page elaborates on the coverage, integration, and powers of the RBI Integrated Ombudsman Scheme. It then introduces and explains several important RBI initiatives and terms like Standing Deposit Facility (SDF), Variable Rate Reverse Repo (VRRR), Switch Auction, and Co-lending norms. This is highly dense and relevant content for UPSC.

pages.subtopic: Payment Systems, RBI Initiatives, and NBFC Regulation
pages.rationale: This page details co-lending norms, explains Payment Aggregators and their regulation, describes Ways and Means Advances (WMA) including its features and legal framework, and introduces new RBI initiatives like Unified Lending Interface (ULI), CBDC retail sandbox, and Tokenisation of Certificate of Deposits (CD). It concludes by introducing Self-Regulatory Organisations for NBFCs. This is very dense and relevant content.
